channel.title: AI News Feed
channel.description: Curated AI news with Claude-powered summaries

item.title: AI companies want to harvest improv actors’ skills to train AI on human emotion
item.description: If you've got strong creative instincts, the ability to authentically portray emotion, and are capable of staying true to a character's voice throughout a scene, there's a job listing calling for your

item.title: Microsoft’s Copilot AI assistant is coming to current-gen Xbox consoles this year
item.description: Xbox is getting ready to launch its Gaming Copilot AI assistant on "current-generation consoles" this year, according to a report from GamesRadar. Sonali Yadav, Xbox's product manager for gaming AI, r

item.title: Facebook Marketplace adds AI auto-replies for annoying ‘Is this still available?’ messages
item.description: Facebook Marketplace is adding a bunch of new AI-powered tools that are supposed to make selling items on the platform a little more efficient. One feature will use Meta AI to automatically respond to

item.title: Gemini’s task automation is here and it’s wild
item.description:
Computers ordering cappuccinos.
A couple of weeks ago, Google and Samsung announced a big Gemini development coming to their newest devices: task automation. Starting with food delivery and rideshar

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude AI can respond with charts, diagrams, and other visuals now
item.description: Anthropic's latest update to Claude will allow the AI chatbot to generate custom charts, diagrams, and other visualizations during your conversation. If Claude determines a visual is useful based on t

item.title: Anthropic doesn’t trust the Pentagon, and neither should you
item.description: Today we’re talking about the messy, fast-moving situation at Anthropic, the maker of Claude that now finds itself in a very ugly legal battle with the Pentagon. The back-and-forth is complicated, bu

item.title: Bespoke AI models are the next big thing in filmmaking
item.description: Though many AI boosters have convinced themselves that the technology can spit out films and television series whole cloth, claims of Hollywood being cooked feel very premature when you see what peopl

item.title: Microsoft’s Copilot Health can connect to your medical records and wearables
item.description: Microsoft announced on Thursday that it's launching Copilot Health, a "separate, secure space" in Copilot for asking questions about lab results and medical records, searching for providers, analyzing

item.title: You can now ask Google Maps ‘complex, real-world questions’ — and Gemini will answer
item.description: Google is continuing to weave Gemini into the firmament of its most-used products. Today, it announced that Google Maps was getting a new AI-powered "Ask Maps" feature that allows for "complex, real-w

item.title: Perplexity’s Personal Computer turns your spare Mac into an AI agent
item.description: Perplexity wants to be more than just an answer engine. On Wednesday, it launched Personal Computer, a new AI agent tool that can turn a spare Mac into a locally run AI system, pitching it as "a digit

item.title: Why physical AI is becoming manufacturing’s next advantage
item.description: For decades, manufacturers have pursued automation to drive efficiency, reduce costs, and stabilize operations. That approach delivered meaningful gains, but it is no longer enough. Today’s manufactur

item.title: The Download: how AI is used for military targeting, and the Pentagon’s war on Claude
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Defense official reveals how AI chatbots could be used for tar

item.title: Future AI chips could be built on glass
item.description: Human-made glass is thousands of years old. But it’s now poised to find its way into the AI chips used in the world’s newest and largest data centers. This year, a South Korean company called Absolics

item.title: A defense official reveals how AI chatbots could be used for targeting decisions
item.description: The US military might use generative AI systems to rank lists of targets and make recommendations—which would be vetted by humans—about which to strike first, according to a Defense Department officia

item.title: The Download: Early adopters cash in on China’s OpenClaw craze, and US batteries slump
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze In Janua

item.title: Pragmatic by design: Engineering AI for the real world
item.description: The impact of artificial intelligence extends far beyond the digital world and into our everyday lives, across the cars we drive, the appliances in our homes, and medical devices that keep people aliv

item.title: Brutal times for the US battery industry
item.description: Just a few years ago, the battery industry was hot, hot, hot. There was a seemingly infinite number of companies popping up, with shiny new chemistries and massive fundraising rounds. My biggest probl

item.title: Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze
item.description: Feng Qingyang had always hoped to launch his own company, but he never thought this would be how—or that the day would come this fast. Feng, a 27-year-old software engineer based in Beijing, started

item.title: The Download: Pokémon Go to train world models, and the US-China race to find aliens
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How Pokémon Go is giving delivery robots an inch-perfect view

item.title: Building a strong data infrastructure for AI agent success
item.description: In the race to adopt and show value from AI, enterprises are moving faster than ever to deploy agentic AI as copilots, assistants, and autonomous task-runners. In late 2025, nearly two-thirds of compa

item.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
item.description: Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B fundi

item.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
item.description: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive. Claude Code, Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the

item.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
item.description: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs, needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $100 million offers seemed impossible. So he spent $5,

item.title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
item.description: Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot, the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification tool into what executives describe as a fully powe

item.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
item.description: Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users — and according to company insiders, the team bui

item.title: Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
item.description: Nous Research, the open-source artificial intelligence startup backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm, released a new competitive programming model on Monday that it says matches or exceeds several la

item.title: The creator of Claude Code just revealed his workflow, and developers are losing their minds
item.description: When the creator of the world's most advanced coding agent speaks, Silicon Valley doesn't just listen — it takes notes. For the past week, the engineering community has been dissecting a thread on X f

item.title: Built a tiny edge-based tracker for AI/LLM crawlers on my Astro blog
item.description: Article URL: https://xergioalex.com/blog/tracking-invisible-ai-bot-analytics/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47256717 Points: 1 # Comments: 0

item.title: Ask HN: Anyone Using a Mac Studio for Local AI/LLM?
item.description: Curious to know your experience running local LLM's with a well spec'ed out M3 Ultra or M4 Pro Mac Studio. I don't see a lot of discussion on the Mac Studio for Local LLMs but it seems like you could
